cohorts.stratified: One app from each category, selected before manual review.
cohorts.adversarial: Ten low-signal, collision-prone, gated, or contradictory results flagged from the raw run.

method.scoring: One point only when a provider-owned source directly supports the field. Unknown or indirect evidence scores zero.
method.remainingUncertainty: Four claims remain intentionally medium-confidence: Fanbasis access breadth, Paygent documentation breadth, and iPayX auth/API availability.

samples.note: Attio auth, REST surface, webhooks, and official MCP were directly documented.
Intercom's private-token, public OAuth, REST, and remote MCP paths matched docs.
Slack's API result was sound; review added the 2026 official MCP and admin-install constraint.
Google Ads requires OAuth plus a developer token and reviewed production access.
BigCommerce documents REST, GraphQL, OAuth tokens, dev stores, and hosted MCP.
Ahrefs key/OAuth paths and plan-gated production access were supported.
GitHub REST, GraphQL, token types, and official MCP were directly supported.
Notion internal tokens, public OAuth, REST, and hosted MCP matched docs.
Stripe API keys, Connect OAuth, REST, and hosted MCP matched docs.
Otter's 2026 Enterprise REST API and OAuth-authenticated MCP were verified in its Help Center.
Close retrieved Stripe and monday.com sources. Entity locking replaced the entire result with Close docs.
Plain also retrieved unrelated MCP vendors. Provider-domain enforcement recovered Plain's GraphQL API.
systeme.io auth/API were right, but a community GitHub MCP was mislabeled as first-party.
Fanbasis search relied on unofficial packages and Zapier MCP. Official docs are login-gated, so MCP was reset to none found.
Sherlock resolved to an LCL payment product. The assignment target is the open-source username CLI.
Paygent Connect resolved to an unrelated AI-billing product. The product site supports REST, bearer keys, webhooks, and contact sales.
iPayX was conflated with IXOPAY. No public developer docs were located, so auth/API remain unknown rather than inferred.
The agent checked consumer NotebookLM only; review found Gemini Notebook Enterprise's licensed preview API.
Mermaid CLI drifted to the separate Mermaid Chart SaaS. The target is a local MIT CLI with no auth.
The agent researched youtubetranscript.dev instead of transcriptapi.com. Domain locking fixed API and MCP evidence.
